message:
feat(demo_showcase): shell-out action for terminal release/restore (bd-194c)

Implements showcase-grade example of "drop to shell" pattern:
- New shell_action.rs module with pager helper functions
- Uses bubbletea::sequence to chain release_terminal/restore_terminal
- Respects $PAGER env var, falls back to less/more/prompt
- Headless-safe: no-op in self-check mode for CI compatibility
- Keyboard shortcut 'D' opens diagnostics in external pager
- Diagnostics shows version, env vars, platform info

Also fixes:
- jobs.rs: Cmd::message -> Cmd::new for job action notifications

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15xi","title":"Logs: copy/export + clear actions","description":"High-polish actions for the Logs page.

Why:
- Real ops tools treat logs as data you can *use* (export, copy, inspect), not just a scrolling wall.
- This is also a great way to demo bubbletea effect composition (batch/sequence) and "real product" ergonomics.

Actions to implement:
- Copy:
  - copy current selected line (if selection exists)
  - copy visible window (current viewport slice)
  - if clipboard integration is not available in all environments, fall back to writing to a file and show the path
- Export:
  - export the full log buffer to a file
  - output location should be deterministic/test-friendly:
    - in E2E/self-check mode: write under `target/demo_showcase_e2e/` (or a Config-provided artifacts dir)
    - otherwise: write to a sensible user-facing path (e.g., `./demo_showcase_exports/`)
  - prefer a stable plain-text format; optionally provide a richer styled export if feasible
- Inspect in pager (stretch):
  - open the exported log file in `$PAGER` using the shell-out flow (release terminal -> run -> restore)
- Clear:
  - clear the buffer (with a confirm if needed) and show a toast

Implementation guidance:
- Use `bubbletea::batch(...)` for "toast + log entry + state update".
- Use the shared shell-out action for pager integration (no ad-hoc terminal hacks).
- All failures must become toasts/log entries, never panics.

Acceptance:
- Actions are fast, obvious, and feel like a real tool.
- No terminal artifacts (cursor/raw/alt-screen) after running actions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:41.355134177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:43.437949348Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:07.823273731Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:43.437920845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:09.126120827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:41.394965242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-9zov","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:46.950082977Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:44.337497742Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-17p2","title":"huh: Fix MultiSelect cursor position mismatch with filtering","description":"# Bug: MultiSelect Cursor Position Not Adjusted When Filter Changes

## Problem Statement
The MultiSelect field has infrastructure for filtering (filter_value field, filtered_options() method)
but does NOT handle cursor adjustment when the filter changes.

## Current Code Analysis

### Cursor is in filtered-space (CORRECT)
The cursor correctly operates in filtered-space:
- Navigation bounds: `if self.cursor < filtered.len().saturating_sub(1)` ✓
- Toggle selection: `filtered.get(self.cursor)` ✓
- View comparison: `self.offset + i == self.cursor` ✓

### Missing: Cursor Adjustment on Filter Change
There is NO code that:
1. Handles filter input (updating filter_value)
2. Adjusts cursor when filter changes
3. Keeps cursor pointed at same item when filter results change

## Example of the Bug
\`\`\`
Initial: options = [A, B, C, D, E], no filter
cursor = 2 (pointing at C in filtered-space, which = C)

Filter applied: "a" (matches A only)
filtered = [(0, A)]
cursor = 2 (STILL 2, but filtered only has 1 item!)

Bug: cursor points past end of filtered list
- toggle_current() → filtered.get(2) → None → no toggle!
- view() → is_cursor = (offset + 0 == 2) → false → nothing highlighted!
\`\`\`

## Root Cause
When filter_value changes (once input handling is added), the code needs to:
1. Remap cursor to track the same item (if it's still visible)
2. OR clamp cursor to valid range (if current item is filtered out)

## Solution Design

### When Filter Changes
\`\`\`rust
fn update_filter_value(&mut self, new_value: String) {
    let old_filtered = self.filtered_options();
    let current_item_idx = old_filtered.get(self.cursor).map(|(idx, _)| *idx);
    
    self.filter_value = new_value;
    
    let new_filtered = self.filtered_options();
    
    // Try to keep cursor on same item
    if let Some(item_idx) = current_item_idx {
        if let Some(new_pos) = new_filtered.iter().position(|(idx, _)| *idx == item_idx) {
            self.cursor = new_pos;
            self.adjust_offset();
            return;
        }
    }
    
    // Item no longer visible, clamp cursor
    self.cursor = self.cursor.min(new_filtered.len().saturating_sub(1));
    self.adjust_offset();
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation Steps
1. Add filter input handling to update() method (KeyType::Runes)
2. Add update_filter_value() method with cursor adjustment
3. Add adjust_offset() to keep cursor visible after filter change
4. Add filter display in view() (show current filter text)
5. Add Backspace handling to remove filter chars
6. Add Escape handling to clear filter

## Testing Strategy (Comprehensive)
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn multiselect_cursor_stays_on_item_when_filter_narrows() {
    let mut ms = MultiSelect::new(vec!["Apple", "Apricot", "Banana", "Cherry"])
        .filterable(true);
    ms.cursor = 2; // Banana
    
    ms.update_filter_value("a".to_string());
    
    assert_eq!(ms.cursor, 2); // Still at Banana (now index 2 in filtered)
    assert_eq!(ms.filtered_options()[ms.cursor].1.key, "Banana");
}

#[test]
fn multiselect_cursor_clamps_when_item_hidden() {
    let mut ms = MultiSelect::new(vec!["Apple", "Banana", "Cherry"])
        .filterable(true);
    ms.cursor = 1; // Banana
    
    ms.update_filter_value("c".to_string()); // Only Cherry
    
    assert_eq!(ms.cursor, 0); // Clamped to valid range
}

#[test]
fn multiselect_filter_then_toggle() {
    let mut ms = MultiSelect::new(vec!["Cat", "Car", "Dog"])
        .filterable(true);
    
    ms.update_filter_value("ca".to_string());
    ms.cursor = 1; // Car in filtered list
    
    ms.toggle_current();
    assert!(ms.selected.contains(&1)); // Original index of Car
}

#[test]
fn multiselect_filter_navigation_bounds() {
    let mut ms = MultiSelect::new(vec!["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]);
    ms.update_filter_value("a".to_string()); // Only A
    ms.cursor = 0;
    
    // Down should not move past end
    let filtered = ms.filtered_options();
    if ms.cursor < filtered.len().saturating_sub(1) {
        ms.cursor += 1;
    }
    assert_eq!(ms.cursor, 0); // Still 0, only 1 item
}
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: multiselect_filter_e2e.sh
# Simulates user filtering and selecting in MultiSelect

echo "=== MultiSelect Filter E2E Test ==="
cargo test --package huh multiselect_filter -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/multiselect_filter.log

if grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/multiselect_filter.log; then
    echo "[PASS] MultiSelect filter tests passed"
else
    echo "[FAIL] MultiSelect filter tests failed - see /tmp/multiselect_filter.log"
    exit 1
fi
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Filter input handling added (typing filters options)
- [ ] Cursor tracks same item when filter narrows
- [ ] Cursor clamps when current item is filtered out
- [ ] offset adjusted to keep cursor visible
- [ ] toggle_current() works correctly with filter
- [ ] Navigation respects filtered bounds
- [ ] Comprehensive unit test coverage
- [ ] E2E test script added to tests/e2e/","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:39.853210637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:07:43.036634995Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:07:43.036568100Z","close_reason":"Implemented filter cursor tracking with 4 tests, committed as 5d4711d","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cursor","filtering","huh","multiselect"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17p2","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:19:39.874689556Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-194c","title":"demo_showcase core: shell-out action (release/restore terminal + Cmd::sequence demo)","description":"Add a *showcase-grade* example of temporarily giving the user their terminal back, running a real command, then restoring the TUI.

Why this matters:
- This is a signature Bubble Tea capability ("drop to shell" / run external tools) that makes TUIs feel like real products.
- It exercises bubbletea’s terminal lifecycle helpers and demonstrates *how to do side effects correctly*.
- It provides a practical escape hatch for users: open a pager, dump diagnostics, run a quick command, then return.

What to build:
- A reusable helper that returns a bubbletea `Cmd` using `bubbletea::sequence(...)` to:
  1) `bubbletea::screen::release_terminal()` (restore cooked mode, show cursor, leave alt-screen if enabled)
  2) run an external command (prefer `$PAGER`, fallback to a built-in "press Enter to return" prompt)
  3) `bubbletea::screen::restore_terminal()` and force a full redraw
- At least one in-app entrypoint to trigger it (pick one):
  - Command palette action (preferred if present)
  - Settings/About "Open diagnostics in pager" action
  - Logs "Open selection in pager" action

Design constraints:
- Must be deterministic and CI-safe:
  - In `--self-check` / custom-IO mode, the action must NOT hang waiting for a pager.
  - In headless mode, it should instead log what *would* have happened and no-op.
- Avoid platform brittleness:
  - If spawning a pager, keep it optional and gracefully handle missing `$PAGER` / non-zero exit.

Acceptance:
- Triggering the action never leaves the terminal broken (cursor, raw mode, alt screen all restored).
- The implementation uses `bubbletea::sequence` (no ad-hoc sleeps; no manual "state machine" for this flow).
- Failure modes are user-friendly: errors become toasts/logs rather than panics.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:49.242205979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:13.199009242Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:33.088580151Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:31.777567228Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:30.460167582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-194c","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:49.273219579Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1aad","title":"demo_showcase core: help overlay + key hints","description":"Implement an in-app Help overlay that makes the demo self-teaching.

Why:
- A flagship demo must be usable without reading source.
- Help is also where we can advertise advanced features (mouse, focus, paste, command palette).

Requirements:
- Trigger:
  - `?` toggles the overlay
  - `esc` closes
- Content:
  - global keybindings (navigation, quit, help, focus, theme)
  - current page keybindings (contextual section)
  - small notes for mouse + paste behavior (if enabled)
- Presentation:
  - use `bubbles::help` and/or a custom lipgloss-rendered modal
  - scrollable if content exceeds height
  - looks like part of the product, not a debug dump

Integration:
- Source the keymap from the canonical UX keybindings definition so the overlay cannot drift.

Acceptance:
- Easy to extend as new features/pages are added.

 {"id":"bd-244a","title":"glamour: Add LRU cache limit to StyleCache","description":"# Task: Add Size Limit to StyleCache

## Problem Statement
The StyleCache in syntax.rs uses unbounded Vec storage:

\`\`\`rust
pub struct StyleCache {
    cache: Vec<(SynStyle, LipglossStyle)>,  // No size limit!
}

impl StyleCache {
    pub fn get_or_convert(&mut self, syn_style: &SynStyle) -> &LipglossStyle {
        // Linear search O(n)
        if let Some(idx) = self.cache.iter().position(|(s, _)| s == syn_style) {
            return &self.cache[idx].1;
        }
        // Add new entry - unbounded growth
        self.cache.push((syn_style.clone(), lip_style));
        &self.cache.last().unwrap().1
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Impact Analysis
- **Memory**: Large code files with many unique styles accumulate entries
- **Performance**: Linear search degrades as cache grows
- **No cleanup**: Entries never removed, even for completed documents

## Solution Design

### Use LRU Cache with Fixed Size
\`\`\`rust
use lru::LruCache;
use std::num::NonZeroUsize;

pub struct StyleCache {
    cache: LruCache<SynStyle, LipglossStyle>,
}

impl StyleCache {
    const DEFAULT_CAPACITY: usize = 256;
    
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(Self::DEFAULT_CAPACITY).unwrap()),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn with_capacity(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(capacity.max(1)).unwrap()),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn get_or_convert(&mut self, syn_style: &SynStyle) -> LipglossStyle {
        if let Some(style) = self.cache.get(syn_style) {
            return style.clone();
        }
        let lip_style = convert_style(syn_style);
        self.cache.put(syn_style.clone(), lip_style.clone());
        lip_style
    }
    
    /// Returns the current number of cached entries.
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.len()
    }
    
    /// Returns true if the cache is empty.
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
        self.cache.is_empty()
    }
    
    /// Clears all cached entries.
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.cache.clear();
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Why LRU?
- Simple API, well-tested crate
- O(1) get and put operations
- Automatic eviction of least-recently-used entries
- Common styles (comments, keywords) stay cached

## Implementation Steps
1. Add \`lru\` crate as dependency
2. Replace Vec with LruCache
3. Update get_or_convert to use LRU API (returns clone, not reference)
4. Add configurable capacity
5. Add len(), is_empty(), clear() methods
6. Add benchmarks comparing old vs new
7. Test cache eviction behavior

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod style_cache_tests {
    use super::*;

    fn make_style(id: u8) -> SynStyle {
        SynStyle {
            foreground: syntect::highlighting::Color { r: id, g: 0, b: 0, a: 255 },
            background: syntect::highlighting::Color::WHITE,
            font_style: syntect::highlighting::FontStyle::empty(),
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_default_capacity() {
        let cache = StyleCache::new();
        assert_eq!(cache.capacity(), 256);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_custom_capacity() {
        let cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(100);
        // Note: actual capacity might be slightly different due to LRU internals
        assert!(cache.capacity() >= 100);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_respects_limit() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(3);
        
        // Add 4 different styles
        for i in 0..4 {
            let style = make_style(i);
            cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        }
        
        // Cache should have exactly 3 entries (oldest evicted)
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_hit_returns_same_style() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(10);
        let style = make_style(42);
        
        let first = cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        let second = cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        
        // Should return identical styles
        assert_eq!(first, second);
    }

    #[test]
    fn lru_eviction_behavior() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(2);
        
        let s1 = make_style(1);
        let s2 = make_style(2);
        let s3 = make_style(3);
        
        cache.get_or_convert(&s1);  // [s1]
        cache.get_or_convert(&s2);  // [s1, s2]
        cache.get_or_convert(&s1);  // [s2, s1] - s1 moved to back (recently used)
        cache.get_or_convert(&s3);  // [s1, s3] - s2 evicted (LRU)
        
        // s1 should still be cached (recently used)
        // s2 should be evicted (least recently used)
        // s3 should be cached (just added)
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 2);
        
        // Verify by checking if lookup requires conversion
        // (This is an indirect test - ideally track cache misses)
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_clear() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(10);
        cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(1));
        cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(2));
        
        assert!(!cache.is_empty());
        
        cache.clear();
        
        assert!(cache.is_empty());
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn many_unique_styles_bounded() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(100);
        
        // Add 1000 unique styles
        for i in 0u8..=255 {
            for j in 0u8..4 {
                let style = SynStyle {
                    foreground: syntect::highlighting::Color { r: i, g: j, b: 0, a: 255 },
                    background: syntect::highlighting::Color::WHITE,
                    font_style: syntect::highlighting::FontStyle::empty(),
                };
                cache.get_or_convert(&style);
            }
        }
        
        // Cache should be bounded at capacity
        assert!(cache.len() <= 100);
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Benchmark Tests
\`\`\`rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion, BenchmarkId};

fn bench_cache_hits(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(256);
    let style = make_style(42);
    cache.get_or_convert(&style);  // Prime cache
    
    c.bench_function("cache_hit", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            cache.get_or_convert(&style)
        });
    });
}

fn bench_cache_misses(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(256);
    let mut counter = 0u8;
    
    c.bench_function("cache_miss", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            counter = counter.wrapping_add(1);
            cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(counter))
        });
    });
}

fn bench_cache_under_pressure(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("cache_pressure");
    
    for capacity in [16, 64, 256, 1024] {
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("lookup", capacity),
            &capacity,
            |b, &cap| {
                let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(cap);
                let mut counter = 0u8;
                
                b.iter(|| {
                    counter = counter.wrapping_add(1);
                    cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(counter))
                });
            },
        );
    }
    group.finish();
}

criterion_group!(benches, bench_cache_hits, bench_cache_misses, bench_cache_under_pressure);
criterion_main!(benches);
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: glamour_style_cache_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== glamour StyleCache LRU Tests ==="

# Run unit tests
echo "Running unit tests..."
cargo test --package glamour style_cache -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/style_cache.log

# Run benchmarks
echo ""
echo "Running benchmarks..."
cargo bench --package glamour -- cache 2>&1 | tee /tmp/style_cache_bench.log

# Memory check (if valgrind available)
if command -v valgrind &> /dev/null; then
    echo ""
    echo "Running memory check..."
    valgrind --leak-check=full cargo test --package glamour style_cache_memory 2>&1 | tee /tmp/style_cache_mem.log
    
    if grep -q "definitely lost: 0 bytes" /tmp/style_cache_mem.log; then
        echo "[PASS] No memory leaks detected"
    else
        echo "[WARN] Potential memory issues - review /tmp/style_cache_mem.log"
    fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Results ==="
if grep -q "FAILED" /tmp/style_cache.log; then
    echo "[FAIL] Some tests failed"
    exit 1
else
    echo "[PASS] All StyleCache tests passed"
fi
\`\`\`

## Performance Notes
- LRU cache uses HashMap internally: O(1) operations
- Small overhead for maintaining LRU order
- Memory usage now bounded: capacity * sizeof(style) bytes max
- Default capacity 256 should cover most syntax themes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StyleCache uses LRU with configurable capacity
- [ ] Default capacity of 256 entries
- [ ] O(1) lookup performance maintained
- [ ] Memory usage bounded

 {"id":"bd-2q95","title":"demo_showcase: runtime Config struct + clap/env mapping","description":"Implement the *actual* runtime configuration layer for demo_showcase.

Rationale:
- Many features need toggles (theme, no-color, no-animations, mouse, seed, alt-screen, syntax).
- If every feature reads clap/env directly, the code becomes untestable and inconsistent.

Deliverables:
- `Config` struct representing all runtime options with explicit defaults.
- CLI parsing (clap) that maps args -> Config.
- Environment variable integration + precedence rules (e.g., `NO_COLOR` overrides unless `--force-color`, etc.).
- Validation (e.g., widths > 0, seed parse, theme name exists).
- Export the resolved config in About/Diagnostics.

Must cover options mentioned across the plan:
- `--theme <name|auto>` + optional `--theme-file <path>`
- `--seed <u64>`
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (so bubbletea println/printf can be demonstrated cleanly)
- `--self-check` (headless)
- Files/fixtures root (for Files page + E2E determinism): `--files-root <path>` (name TBD)
- SSH mode args (if enabled): addr, host key
- Syntax toggles (if enabled): syntax on/off, line numbers

Acceptance:
- There is a single source of truth for runtime toggles.
- Unit tests can construct Config directly without parsing CLI.
- CLI parsing is thin and well-logged.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:56.461191204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:41:40.548492408Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:41:40.548426525Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:07.927570580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:06.656528955Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q95","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:56.477231837Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qx1","title":"demo_showcase page: Logs (styled + filterable)","description":"A live log viewer page that feels like a production-grade log tailer.

Why:
- Logging is where real apps live and die; this is the most convincing place to show `charmed_log` styling + ergonomics.
- It also provides the “ground truth” for cross-page workflows: Jobs should link to logs; errors should explain themselves.

Must showcase:
- `charmed_log`-styled output:
  - level coloring (trace/debug/info/warn/error)
  - structured fields (service, job_id, request_id, duration, user)
  - readable formatting even at narrow widths
- Bubbles viewport:
  - smooth scrolling, jump to top/bottom
  - follow mode (“tail -f”) that pauses when the user scrolls up
  - optional line wrapping toggle
- Filtering/search:
  - filter by level and service
  - query search with match highlight + next/prev navigation
  - correlation filters (e.g., “show logs for selected job”)
- Actions:
  - copy selected range or last N lines
  - export to a file (with a clear artifact path) and show toast confirmation
  - clear/reset view (without breaking follow mode)

Data requirements:
- Log stream is deterministic in test mode and correlated with the Jobs simulation.
- Includes occasional warnings/errors so filtering and drill-down are meaningful.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a real log tailer (usable, not flashy-for-flashy’s-sake).
- Filtering is instant and predictable; follow mode never fights the user.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filter + search correctness (stable ordering, edge cases)
  - follow mode logic (scroll up pauses, scroll bottom resumes)
  - export/copy behavior produces expected artifacts/messages
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - logs copy/export/clear scenario (verifies artifacts + JSONL logs)

Notes:
- Avoid “fake rainbow logs”; prioritize legibility and density.
- Ensure `--no-color` / ASCII modes still produce clear output.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.489282051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:56.936627Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qx1","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.508975562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+// ============================================================================
+// Shell-Out Action (bd-194c)
+// ============================================================================
+
+/// Messages for shell-out (terminal release/restore) actions.
+///
+/// This enables temporarily giving the user their terminal back to run
+/// external commands, then restoring the TUI. A signature capability that
+/// makes TUIs feel like real products.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+#[allow(dead_code)] // Variants are part of the public API for programmatic use
+pub enum ShellOutMsg {
+    /// Request to open diagnostics in an external pager.
+    OpenDiagnostics,
+    /// The pager command completed (success or with error message).
+    PagerCompleted(Option<String>),
+    /// Terminal has been released for external use.
+    TerminalReleased,
+    /// Terminal has been restored after external use.
+    TerminalRestored,
+}
+
+impl ShellOutMsg {
+    /// Create a bubbletea Message from this `ShellOutMsg`.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn into_message(self) -> Message {
+        Message::new(self)
+    }
+}
